events.name: Spring Plot Assignment Day
events.description: Annual lottery and assignment of raised-bed plots for the 2026 growing season. Returning gardeners confirm their plots and new applicants draw from the waitlist. Soil amendment kits distributed to all confirmed gardeners.

events.name: Seed Swap and Season Kickoff
events.description: Gardeners bring surplus seeds and starts to trade. Master gardener from Penn State Extension gives a short talk on companion planting for Philadelphia row-lot micro-plots. Light refreshments provided by the CDC.
events.rain_plan: Indoor at CDC office, same time

events.name: Summer Solstice Potluck
events.description: Community potluck celebrating the longest day. Each gardener brings a dish and the CDC supplies lemonade, plates, and utensils. Live acoustic music from the Fishtown Folk Circle. Kid-friendly activities at the south end of the lot.
events.rain_plan: Move to Kensington Rec Center gymnasium

events.name: Mid-Season Compost Workshop
events.description: Hands-on workshop covering hot composting, worm bin maintenance, and proper carbon-to-nitrogen ratios. Participants take home a starter worm kit. Co-facilitated by Philadelphia Compost Alliance.
events.rain_plan: Indoor at CDC office, same time

events.name: Youth Garden Day
events.description: Morning program for neighborhood kids ages 6-14. Planting a fall crop row, painting garden markers, and a scavenger hunt. Parents must sign a waiver and stay on site. Snacks provided by CDC and Fishtown Neighbors Association.

events.name: Harvest Festival
events.description: Biggest event of the season. Gardeners display their harvest for the community. Friendly competition for largest tomato, most creative salsa, and best container garden. Local food trucks on E. Allegheny. Donations accepted for the Kensington Pantry.

events.name: Fall Soil Testing Clinic
events.description: Penn State Extension collects soil samples from each plot for pH, lead, and nutrient analysis. Results mailed within three weeks. Free for all registered gardeners. Bring a labeled ziplock per plot.

events.name: Winterization Workday
events.description: Full-day volunteer workday to prepare the garden for winter. Tasks include draining the irrigation system, spreading mulch on perennial beds, tarping raised beds, storing summer infrastructure, and cleaning the tool shed. Porter & Koval Plumbing provides plumbing winterization support. Target headcount is 38 volunteers. Waivers required. Coffee and breakfast pastries from 06:00 to 07:00.

events.name: End-of-Season Volunteer Appreciation
events.description: Indoor gathering to thank all 2026 volunteers. Carlos presents season totals: volunteer hours, produce donated to the pantry, and compost generated. Certificates for gardeners who logged 40+ volunteer hours. Emily Porter sponsors the catering through Porter & Koval community budget.

events.name: Holiday Wreath Workshop
events.description: Craft workshop using dried garden materials, grapevine rings, and donated ribbon to make holiday wreaths. Materials fee five dollars per wreath. All proceeds go to the 2027 garden seed fund. Open to the public.
